digest.component_or_area: JWKS/OIDC JWT verifier on the MCP auth seam
propositions: Add a JWKSTokenVerifier selected by the jwt auth strategy that validates OIDC/JWT tokens against a JWKS, checking signature with pinned algorithms, iss, aud==auth_resource_server_url, and exp/iat/nbf with bounded leeway.
The verifier pins algorithms and rejects alg:none, and refuses to start if a symmetric algorithm (HS256/HS384/HS512) is configured alongside a JWKS source to block RS256/HS256 key-confusion attacks.
JWKS fetch is SSRF-safe: jwks_uri is fixed from trusted config, HTTPS-only, private/link-local hosts rejected by default unless auth_jwt_allow_private_jwks_host=true, with a bounded timeout.
An optional configurable typ header check (auth_jwt_expected_typ, e.g. at+JWT) prevents cross-JWT confusion; unset by default it skips the check.
Unknown-kid refetch is capped to at most one JWKS fetch per cooldown window, made concurrency-safe with an asyncio.Lock to avoid a TOCTOU double-fetch under concurrent requests.
The synchronous PyJWKClient JWKS fetch is offloaded via asyncio.to_thread so it never blocks FastMCP's event loop; fetch failures raise (deny) while invalid tokens return None.
